items.headline: Auto sales use proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: A special development permit was under consideration to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real, an existing commercial property in the ECR-C zone. The proposal includes lot repaving, restriping, and landscape upgrades but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: 195-unit transit-oriented apartment complex permitted near Berryessa
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development has been cleared to build at 825 Berryessa Station Way, adding 195 apartments to the Berryessa Transit Village. This is the largest housing project in the batch and directly supports the city's transit-oriented development goals near the BART station.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance pilot for water customers
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new Financial Assistance Pilot Program for Water Customers on May 12, designed to help residents manage water bills. The program details and eligibility requirements were presented to the council for action.

items.headline: Council to discuss potential litigation over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a closed session on May 12 to confer with legal counsel about potential litigation arising from a Cuesta Park water main incident. The council also negotiated real property matters related to properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission considers 27-townhome residential project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a residential development proposal for 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. This mixed-income housing project represents a significant addition to Cupertino's residential stock.

items.headline: Litigation update: Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino case in progress
items.summary: The City Council held a closed session regarding ongoing litigation in case Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino and SummerHill Homes, LLC. An open session report on the closed discussion was made available to the public.

items.headline: Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: Palo Alto will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing development proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city-owned 52-space parking lot. The 72-unit project requests density bonus concessions under state law and is undergoing streamlined review.

items.headline: City Council adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: Campbell City Council adopted a resolution on May 5 that prohibits city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement activities. This policy limits how federal agencies can access or utilize municipal facilities and resources.
